secondaryKeywords: how to buy a warehouse robot
title: Warehouse robot buyer's checklist (2026) + step-by-step guide
metaDescription: Free warehouse robot buyer's checklist: AMR vs AGV, integration budget, 60–90 day pilot, buy vs lease vs RaaS, plus questions to ask vendors.
intro: Use the checklist below before vendor demos — then read the step-by-step guide for problem definition, technology shortlisting, pilot design, and acquisition negotiation. Buying a warehouse robot works best when you start from a specific workflow problem, not from a sales catalog.

sections.heading: Step 1: Define the automation problem
bullets: Transport between zones vs picking vs pallet moves
Orders and picks per day — volume drives ROI
Layout stability: fixed lanes vs frequent changes
WMS readiness: none, partial, or integration-ready

sections.heading: Step 2: Shortlist robot types
paragraphs: Map pain points to robot categories before calling vendors. Transport + changing layouts often points to AMRs; fixed pallet lanes may point to AGVs or pallet movers; picking pain may include pick-assist.

sections.heading: Step 3: Budget beyond hardware
bullets: Training for supervisors and floor staff

sections.heading: Step 4: Run a structured pilot
paragraphs: Plan 60–90 days on one workflow with measurable KPIs: travel time, picks per hour, incident rate, and uptime. RaaS pilots lower risk when utilization is unproven.

sections.heading: Step 5: Negotiate acquisition model
bullets: Buy: best for stable, high-utilization fleets with capital
Lease: spreads cost; watch buyout and service terms
RaaS: OpEx-friendly; confirm SLA, swaps, and buyout options

faqs.question: How long does it take to deploy warehouse robots?
faqs.answer: Small AMR pilots can go live in weeks; AGV infrastructure projects often take months. Integration depth and safety sign-off drive the timeline.

faqs.question: What should I ask vendors in the first call?
faqs.answer: Use the ten questions in the checklist above — reference sites, workflow fit, WMS path, pilot terms, and all-in first-year cost matter more than unit list price alone.

faqs.question: Can I print the warehouse robot buyer's checklist?
faqs.answer: Yes. Click Print checklist (PDF) at the top of the checklist section — it formats a one-page summary you can share with ops and procurement.
